prompt: Implement a Regional Tourism Resource & Lodging Operations Portal for destination marketing organizations and affiliated clinics that need to manage public-facing travel resources while maintaining controlled access for operational staff. The Yew-based web UI (English-only) provides role-tailored navigation for Administrator, Publisher, Reviewer, Clinician, and Inventory Clerk roles, using local username/password login and optional configurable MFA via offline TOTP codes. Users can build and publish a structured resource library of attractions, routes, events, and services with categories, tags, hours, pricing, contact info, media, and geo-coordinates entered as a US address plus optional latitude/longitude; each item follows draft → in review → published → offline states, supports version history, and can be scheduled to publish at a specific local time (e.g., 03/27/2026 9:00 PM). In the same portal, staff manage lodging-style listings with a full lifecycle: publish/edit, online/offline controls, amenity maintenance, vacancy and leaseable period calendars (minimum 7 nights, maximum 365 nights), and rent/deposit rules (deposit capped at 1.5× monthly rent, rent changes require reviewer approval). Media uploads accept only JPG/PNG/MP4 under 50 MB per file with immediate client-side previews and clear inline validation errors. Operational users can also track on-hand supplies tied to facilities using warehouse/bin locations, lot numbers, and expiration dates; the UI highlights near-expiry inventory within 30 days, supports reserving quantities against allocations, and provides searchable inbound/outbound transaction history with printable audit views. The backend uses Actix-web to expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the Yew frontend, enforcing RBAC down to menu visibility and data scope (e.g., a Clinician only sees their facility, an Administrator can see all). PostgreSQL persists resource/library content, listing lifecycle states, review decisions, scheduling metadata, inventory lots/expirations, and immutable transaction logs; all writes are validated server-side with deterministic rules and compliance checks (required fields, numeric bounds, allowed state transitions, and review gates). Data integration and batch processing run fully offline: bulk Excel import/export supports up to 10,000 rows per job with a local job queue, progress percent, resumable retries (max 3), failure logs, and rollback by staging imports into temporary tables before a single commit. “API integration” is implemented as local-network connectors only: REST endpoints and an optional on-prem message queue interface for Academic Affairs/SSO/Student Information Systems equivalents, with request signing, nonce+timestamp anti-replay (5-minute window), and idempotency keys to prevent duplicate ingestion. Security includes TLS for internal deployments using locally managed certificates, encryption at rest for sensitive columns (password hashes with Argon2id; contact details encrypted), CSRF/XSS/injection protections, strict upload allowlists with file-type sniffing and checksum validation, and minimum data collection with masking in screens and exports (e.g., phone shown as (415) ***-1234). Exports of sensitive datasets require a second-person approval and can apply an optional watermark containing username and timestamp. Operations are handled through a configuration center with environment-specific parameters, feature switches, dictionaries, and maintenance windows; Actix health checks and Prometheus-compatible metrics are exposed on the local network, with exception alerts written to local logs and canary release supported via multi-environment configuration profiles without any dependency on external services.
